response.justification: While Bogdan brings 20 years of software development experience and strong expertise in distributed systems, microservices architecture, and infrastructure tooling (Kubernetes, AWS CDK), he falls significantly short on two critical minimum qualifications: C++ experience (6 months at National Instruments vs. required 8 years) and explicit technical leadership in team mentoring/management roles. His background is strongest in full-stack web and infrastructure tooling rather than low-level systems programming or cloud compute platform development.
strengths: Extensive infrastructure and distributed systems experience: led microservices handling millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby, deployed Kubernetes platforms, proficient with AWS CDK
Proven ability to establish engineering best practices: organized refactoring dojos, created TDD/testing workshops, championed CI/CD and trunk-based development practices
Demonstrated success shipping complex products: launched multiple platforms (Direct Mail, GISCollective, iOS/tvOS apps ranking top 10), with 20 years of shipping experience
concerns: Critical C++ experience gap: only ~6 months of explicit C++ work at National Instruments (2014-2015) versus required 8 years, failing a stated minimum qualification
No evidence of technical leadership in team management or mentoring roles: workshops and code kata sessions don't demonstrate managing engineers or establishing architectural direction for teams
Limited cloud compute platform architecture experience: background is primarily in web infrastructure, data integration, and GIS platforms rather than internal cloud compute systems
